arts.ttl: Bristol City’s Liam Manning: ‘Losing Theo will be a hole in my heart for the rest of my life’
arts.sum: Manager opens up on losing his baby son, how close he came to quitting and the Premier League promotion push After victory at Middlesbrough in October, Bristol City’s players headed towards the pocket of the Riverside Stadium housing their supporters. As the away fans sang on loop the name of their head coach, Liam Manning, the squad unravelled a giant red and white banner displaying the words “Fly High Theo” in block capitals. Manning, on leave after the death of his baby son eight days earlier, was watching from a rural cottage 300 miles south, on a much-needed getaway. “I sent a long message to the lads about it afterwards … yeah, lump in the throat,” he says. “The celebrations epitomised everything. It was above football.” It is not the only heavy moment in a raw and moving conversation but the one thing Manning stresses, as he discusses the hardest episode of his life in an interview for the first time, is that this is not a sob story. He takes huge pride in sharing Theo’s name. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: 'No communication' over new Norwich contract - Scotland's Gunn
arts.sum: Norwich City goalkeeper Angus Gunn says there has been "no communication" from the club over a new contract offer.

arts.ttl: Bristol City cult hero insists Robins must shake Championship 'hoodoo' to earn play-off finish
arts.sum: Bristol City cult hero Matt Smith has suggested the Robins need to shake their "hoodoo" against the Championship's biggest clubs to stand a chance in this season's play-off race, after the Reds' draw with Sheffield United . However, the former striker believes the Robins could break into the play-off places if they're able to convert good performances into wins against the likes of Leeds United, Sunderland and Burnley between now and the end of May.

arts.ttl: Young midfielder signs new Norwich contract
arts.sum: Norwich City midfielder Toby Chilvers has signed his first professional contract with the Canaries.

arts.ttl: Bristol City coach highlights 'crazy' turnaround ahead of Robins' clash with Norwich City
arts.sum: "I think we've spoken about it previously, the scheduling is crazy in my opinion how you play at 8:00pm on a Tuesday night and then you've got to go again on a Friday evening," the assistant head coach told Bristol Live after the draw with Sheffield United. Chris Hogg has labelled the turnaround between Bristol City's draw with Sheffield United and the Robins' clash with Norwich City "crazy" ahead of the Canaries' visit to Ashton Gate this Friday.

arts.ttl: 'There's been no communication' - candid Gunn 'worried' about Norwich future
arts.sum: Norwich City goalkeeper Angus Gunn says talks over his contract haven't even started as a Carrow Road exit looms large on the horizon.

arts.sum: Sheffield United are accelerating efforts to sign former Norwich City man Gabriel Sara from Galatasaray before the summer. That’s according to Fanatik, who report that the Sara’s suitors are increasing and Sheffield United are one of them.
